mnemonics.title: Try-Catch-Throw Flow
mnemonics.content: T-C-T: Think Carefully, Throw - Remember: Try code, Catch error, Throw exception

mnemonics.title: Order of Catch Blocks
mnemonics.content: D-B-S: Derived Before Base, Specific Before General - Like catching small fish before big ones

mnemonics.title: Destructor Rule
mnemonics.content: N-T-D: Never Throw in Destructors - DTN: Destructors Throw Never

acronyms.usage: Managing resources using constructors/destructors, works well with exception handling

acronyms.expansion: No Exception - function promises not to throw
acronyms.usage: Used to optimize code and declare functions that won't throw
Provides standard exception classes in C++

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a 'safety zone' where code inside might cause problems. If something goes wrong, you leave this zone immediately.
Think of it as an 'emergency exit' or 'error handler' that catches anyone fleeing the safety zone.
Like unwinding a coil of rope - as exception travels up, each level's local objects are destroyed one by one.
Tree with std::exception as trunk. Branches: logic_error (left), runtime_error (right). Leaves: specific exceptions.

analogies.analogy: Like a fire alarm in a building - when triggered (throw), people evacuate to exits (catch), and the building is checked (stack unwinding).
Like raising your hand in class to signal a problem - you 'throw' your attention to the teacher who 'catches' it.
Like dominoes falling - one falls (exception thrown), knocking down others (destroying local objects) until caught.
Like a 'No Running' sign in a pool - if someone runs (exception thrown), the lifeguard takes action (terminate).
